growth_records.medium: ZRMG (Zymomonas Rich Medium Glucose), no hydrolysate (control)
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: small-scale aerobic batch fermentations in the absence (Figure 1A) or presence of 8% (v/v) batch 2 hydrolysate (Figure 1B). In the presence of hydrolysate, specific ethanol productivity was reduced about three-fold (0.10
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for WT+pJS71 in ZRMG rich medium without hydrolysate, small-scale aerobic batch fermentation, 30C, shaking 200 rpm, fermentation started at 0.1 OD600. Interval used for the calculation stated in Methods as 0-15 h (formula: (ethanol_t2-ethanol_t1)/(t2-t1)/avg(OD600_t1,OD600_t2)). No OD600 endpoint values given so growth rate/doubling time could not be calculated.

growth_records.medium: ZRMG + 8% (v/v) batch 2 miscanthus dilute-acid hydrolysate
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: In the presence of hydrolysate, specific ethanol productivity was reduced about three-fold (0.10 versus 0.27 g/l/h/OD600).
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for WT+pJS71 in ZRMG + 8% v/v batch-2 miscanthus hydrolysate, aerobic batch fermentation, 30C. Methods list this condition's calculation interval as 0-27 h. A separate, later 4-biological-replicate experiment (Figure 6, paired with ZMO1875 overexpression test) reports a different value (0.16 g/L/h/OD600) for the nominally same WT+hydrolysate condition -- recorded as a distinct observation below because it derives from a separate reported measurement set.

growth_records.medium: ZRMG + miscanthus batch hydrolysate (paired comparator for ZMO1875-overexpression fermentation, Figure 6)
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: overexpression of ZMO1875 improved hydrolysate tolerance and increased specific ethanol productivity 2.4-fold (0.38 versus 0.16 g/l/h/OD600)

Methods state the WT+pJS71-in-hydrolysate interval used was 0-27 h; average of four biological replicates.

growth_records.snippet: We found that overexpression of ZMO1875 improved hydrolysate tolerance and increased specific ethanol productivity 2.4-fold (0.38 versus 0.16 g/l/h/OD600)
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for the ZMO1875-overexpression strain (arabinose-induced Pbad-ZMO1875 on plasmid, in ZM4 background) grown with miscanthus batch hydrolysate, aerobic batch fermentation, 30C, average of four biological replicates; calculation interval stated in Methods as 0-18 h.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined liquid minimal medium (NH4+-replete, 15 mM NH4+)

up.method: not numerically reported in this paper; specific glucose uptake rate stated to be LOWER under NH4+-replete than under N2 fixation; numeric MFA input rates sourced externally (J. McKinlay), not tabulated

sec.method: not numerically reported in this paper; specific ethanol production rate stated to be LOWER under NH4+-replete than under N2 fixation; numeric MFA rates sourced externally, not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Martien et al. Metabolic Remodeling during Nitrogen Fixation in Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00987-21. [PMC8594446]
growth_records.snippet: NH4+ upshift conditions were compared to continuous NH4+-replete conditions; doubling times were approximately 3 h during N2 fixation and 2 h under NH4+... grown with 15 mM NH4+ (replete)
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time under NH4+-replete = 2 h. Calculated growth rate mu = ln2/td = 0.693/2.0 = 0.347 h-1 (growth_method=calculated). Temperature 30C (verbatim). pH not measured on this experimental medium; medium pH adjusted to 6-6.5 range at prep, midpoint 6.25 used. Medium composition is the chemically defined minimal medium; experimental replete cultures used 15 mM NH4+ (starter culture used 1 g/L (NH4)2SO4). 13C-tracer variants used 15 g/L glucose with identical growth rates. Glucose uptake and ethanol production reported only qualitatively (lower under NH4+-replete vs N2 fixation); no per-gDW numeric fluxes are given in the paper (numeric MFA rates were supplied externally by J. McKinlay and not tabulated), so rates left null.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined liquid minimal medium, no NH4+ (N2-fixing)

up.method: not numerically reported; specific glucose consumption stated to be INCREASED during N2 fixation relative to NH4+-replete; numeric MFA input rates sourced externally, not tabulated
N2 is the sole N source under N2-fixing regime; no specific fixation rate reported

sec.method: not numerically reported; specific ethanol production stated to be INCREASED during N2 fixation relative to NH4+-replete; numeric MFA rates sourced externally, not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Martien et al. Metabolic Remodeling during Nitrogen Fixation in Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00987-21. [PMC8594446]
growth_records.snippet: doubling times were approximately 3 h during N2 fixation and 2 h under NH4+... during N2 fixation, Z. mobilis exhibits increased specific rates of glucose consumption and ethanol production... nitrogen source was N2 gas (>90% N2 in the anae
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time under N2 fixation = 3 h. Calculated growth rate mu = ln2/td = 0.693/3.0 = 0.231 h-1 (growth_method=calculated). Under N2 fixation Z. mobilis grows slower (lower growth rate/yield) but has higher biomass-specific glucose uptake and ethanol production than NH4+-replete; directions reported qualitatively only, no per-gDW numeric fluxes in paper, so rate values left null. In no-NH4+ medium the 1 g/L (NH4)2SO4 was replaced by 0.2 g/L MgSO4; N2 gas is the sole N source. Temperature 30C. pH taken as prep midpoint 6.25 (medium adjusted to pH 6-6.5).

growth_records.medium: Zymomonas minimal medium (ZMM)

up.method: 13C-glucose isotope-dilution GC-MS; hourly glucose measurement normalized to gDCW

sec.units: nmol_isoprene mmol_glucose^-1 (yield, not a per-time rate)
sec.method: sealed-vial headspace flame ionization spectrometry (FIS), n=4

growth_records.cite: Khana, Daven B. et al. Systematic Analysis of Metabolic Bottlenecks in the Methylerythritol 4-Phosphate (MEP) Pathway of Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00092-23. [PMC10134818]
growth_records.method: reported — growth rates (h^-1) were measured in three biological replicates from hourly OD600 readings, but the numeric values are given only in Supplementary Table S3, which is not present in the fulltext XML; not extractable here.
growth_records.snippet: Background levels of isoprene production (5.6 nmol_isoprene · mmol_glucose^-1) in Z. mobilis overexpressing GFP were consistent with res...
growth_records.notes: Background/control strain (GFP overexpression). Isoprene yield 5.6 ± 0.5 nmol isoprene/mmol glucose (n=4, Table 2) — this is a product-per-substrate yield, NOT a per-gDW/h rate; placed in secretion_rates for visibility with units flagged. Measured glucose consumption rates (mmol_glucose gDCW^-1 h^-1) and growth rates (h^-1) ARE reported by the paper but only in Supplementary Table S3, absent from the fulltext XML, so numeric qS and mu could not be extracted. Media, anaerobic mode, 30°C, and 20 g/L glucose all explicit in Methods. No rate invented.

growth_records.notes: Primary detailed fermentation (Fig 3). Glucose 160 g/L = 888 mM initial; residual 31.3 g/L (174±2 mM); consumed = 128.7 g/L (714 mM). Ethanol final 71.6 g/L (1553 mM) but 10 g/L (~217 mM) was added at start, so net produced = 61.6 g/L (1336 mM). Yield = 61.6/128.7 = 0.479 g/g; molar 1336/714 = 1.87 mol/mol = 94% of theoretical 2.0. Z. mobilis uses Entner-Doudoroff pathway. No pyruvate accumulation observed; authors infer titer limited by substrate uptake not central metabolism. No gDW/specific-rate data.

growth_records.snippet: ethanol ... accounted for 92.6% of the glucose consumed for anaerobic cultures ... Anaerobic cultures did not accumulate gluconate to any appreciable extent (<0.01% of glucose was converted to gluconate).
growth_records.notes: ANAEROBIC control condition, Z. mobilis ZM4, 30C, minimal media, glucose sole C source, batch. Growth measured as OD600 (experimental). growth_rate CALCULATED: paper states cultures 'underwent approximately one doubling during the 2-h time course' at mid-exponential => doubling_time approx 2 h; mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/2 = 0.347 h-1 (rounded 0.35). Ethanol yield 92.6% of glucose consumed (Fig 8C, NMR); gluconate negligible (<0.01%). Absolute uptake/secretion in mmol/gDW/h NOT derivable: paper reports OD600, extracellular mM concentrations and %-of-glucose fractions but no gDW biomass conversion nor time-resolved slope for a defined interval. Native ethanologen converting up to 96% glucose to ethanol. This is a targeted-metabolomics + 13C-tracing study, not a quantitative net-flux 13C-MFA (13C used qualitatively to trace label incorporation into MEP-pathway intermediates), so mfa_fluxes empty.

growth_records.cite: Martien JI et al. Systems-Level Analysis of Oxygen Exposure in Zymomonas mobilis: Implications for Isoprenoid Production. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00284-18. [PMC6372839]
growth_records.snippet: The final extracellular gluconate concentration was 11.5 mM, representing 18% of the glucose consumed ... ethanol ... accounted for ... only 35% for aerobic cultures ... 42% of the glucose consumed by aerobically grown cultures did not corr
growth_records.notes: AEROBIC condition (O2 shift), Z. mobilis ZM4, 30C, minimal media, glucose sole C, batch. Oxygen inhibited both growth and ethanol production; growth 'markedly lower' under aerobic than anaerobic but NO numeric aerobic mu/doubling reported for the 24-h course, so growth_rate/doubling left null (growth_method reported/qualitative). Carbon utilization fractions (Fig 8C, NMR + LC-MS): ethanol 35% of glucose consumed => yield 0.35 x 2 = 0.70 mol/mol; gluconate 18% => 0.18 mol/mol, final 11.5 mM; 42% of glucose unaccounted (CO2 not measured). Gluconate excreted continuously over 24 h (redox balancing via glucose->gluconate diverting electrons to ETC). Acetate and pyruvate increased under aerobic (not %-quantified). Extracellular MEcDP transiently 2 uM by 30 min. Absolute rates in mmol/gDW/h NOT derivable (no gDW biomass, only OD600 and mM). 13C used qualitatively to trace label incorporation, not a quantitative net-flux 13C-MFA.

growth_records.medium: Minimal medium (defined) with 20 g/L glucose

yields.product_yields: ethanol 89% ± 0.04% (fraction of glucose converted to ethanol)

growth_records.cite: Rivera Vazquez, Julio et al. Physiological and metabolic responses of Zymomonas mobilis to lignocellulosic hydrolysate. Microbiology Spectrum (2025). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.00610-25. [PMC12502597]
growth_records.snippet: Control 0.36 h-1; glucose consumption 43.2 ± 2.2 mmol/gCDW/h; ethanol production 77.2 ± 3.9 mmol/gCDW/h; ethanol yield 89% ± 0.04%. Z. mobilis exhibited an average doubling time of 1.7 hours in minimal media.
growth_records.notes: CONTROL / minimal-media condition, Table 1 row 'Control'. NOTE: the paper explicitly states control data were extracted from Reference 46 (Rivera-Vazquez et al., the authors' prior study) — i.e., these control rates are CITED, not newly measured in this paper. Specific growth rate 0.36 h-1 measured from OD600 growth curves; reported average doubling time in minimal media = 1.7 h (mu=ln2/td would give 0.36 h-1 -> td=1.93 h; paper reports 1.7 h). Rates are specific (per gCDW) already; no further arithmetic applied. Anaerobic; 30°C per Materials & Methods.
